description: Official Lados capability pack that orchestrates and validates the Claude + Remotion AI motion-graphics production workflow (Chronixel's Claude + Remotion Blueprint): script intake, scene drafting, creative-direction batching, rendering, layout revision, chroma-key validation, and image insertion.
ownerBoundary: read a script and compute structure metrics
record a Remotion project's chosen starter template
segment a script into a numbered scene plan
lock and rotate a creative direction per scene batch
request a render pass for a batch of scenes
compute scene revision version labels and layout zones
map uploaded images onto named scene elements
mustNotOwn: generating motion graphics visuals or copy (the connected Claude session does this, not this pack)
actually running the Remotion CLI / producing MP4 output
video editing, timeline assembly, transitions, or audio mixing (DaVinci Resolve / Premiere / Final Cut)
actually keying out a chroma background (the NLE's keyer does this, not this pack)
guardrails: This pack orchestrates and validates the Claude + Remotion motion-graphics workflow; it does not itself call an LLM or run Remotion — creative generation happens in the connected Claude session.
Scene revisions must never overwrite an existing scene; version numbers only increase (Scene X V2, V3, ...) and the scene number never changes, per the Scene Revision & Versioning rule.
Creative direction should rotate every scene batch; generate_scene_batch flags (never blocks) a repeated direction so a human can confirm intent.
Chroma-key requests must resolve to a pure #00FF00 background with no gradients or semi-transparent UI, per the blueprint's green-screen rule.

render_scenes is honest about not having a wired render backend — do not build production templates depending on real MP4 output until a render backend is implemented and this pack's runtimeStatus is updated to runtime_enabled.

verification.manifest: draft — new pack, not yet reviewed by eff
verification.runtime: partial — read_script, scaffold_project, draft_scenes, generate_scene_batch, revise_scene_layout, remove_background, insert_images implemented; render_scenes is a stub (no Remotion render backend wired into the API)
verification.templates: draft — script-to-scene-plan added: standard skeleton descriptor plus a real, importable graph body chaining 5 of 8 nodes automatically (3 ad-hoc revision nodes are standalone by design, see templates/README.md); not yet sanity-checked with a real import + trigger
